title: caltha palustris var. laeta
intro: Caltha palustris var. laeta, also known as the marsh marigold, is a variant of the marsh marigold plant, a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The plant's health benefits include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and treating skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Caltha palustris var. laeta can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
